evals.query: Set up Flyway database migrations on startup for a new Kora service: add the dependency, enable the module, and create the first migration with a users table (id, email, created_at).
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:database-flyway dependency alongside ru.tinkoff.kora:database-jdbc
Includes annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" (or ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin) and the kora-parent BOM
Plugs FlywayJdbcDatabaseModule into the @KoraApp interface
Creates src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__*.sql with CREATE TABLE users
Does not rely on classpath autoconfiguration or field injection; uses @KoraApp and constructor injection

evals.query: Configure Flyway in my Kora app to use the location db/migration/custom and validate checksums before migrating.
expected_behavior: Sets flyway.locations = ["db/migration/custom"] in the flyway config section
Sets flyway.validateOnMigrate = true
Keeps the keys within the real FlywayConfig surface (enabled, locations, executeInTransaction, validateOnMigrate, mixed, configurationProperties)
Does not invent non-existent keys such as cleanDisabled or a per-script transactional comment

evals.query: Use Liquibase with SQL changesets in my Kora service instead of Flyway. Set up the dependency, the module, and a master changelog with an orders table and a rollback.
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:database-liquibase plus ru.tinkoff.kora:database-jdbc
Plugs LiquibaseJdbcDatabaseModule into the @KoraApp interface
Sets liquibase.changelog to the master changelog path
Writes a formatted-SQL changelog starting with --liquibase formatted sql and using --changeset <author>:<id> plus --rollback
Does not plug FlywayJdbcDatabaseModule in at the same time

evals.query: My Flyway migration with CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on PostgreSQL fails because it cannot run inside a transaction. How do I fix it in Kora?
expected_behavior: Explains that CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction
Sets flyway.mixed = true so the whole run executes without a transaction
Notes this is controlled by config (mixed / executeInTransaction), not a per-script comment
Stays within real FlywayConfig keys and does not invent a non-Kora migration property

evals.query: On startup my Kora app fails with 'Validation failed. Checksum changed' from Flyway. What went wrong and how do I recover?
expected_behavior: Identifies that an already-applied migration script was edited
Advises never editing applied scripts and adding a new versioned V*.sql instead
Mentions repairing the history out-of-process (Flyway repair) to realign checksums
Treats disabling validateOnMigrate as a last resort, not the recommended fix

evals.query: Our Kora service runs with 5 replicas in Kubernetes and the in-app Flyway migrations race on deploy. What is the recommended migration strategy?
expected_behavior: Recommends running migrations out-of-process for horizontally scaled services
Sets flyway.enabled = false (or removes the module) in the app
Proposes a Kubernetes Job with restartPolicy: Never run once before rollout, or a CI step
Mentions the Flyway Gradle plugin (flywayMigrate) for local development
Does not recommend keeping per-replica startup migrations as the production approach

evals.query: How do I run my db/migration Flyway scripts against a throwaway Postgres in Kora integration tests?
expected_behavior: Uses the io.goodforgod:testcontainers-extensions-postgres extension with @TestcontainersPostgreSQL
Declares @Migration(engine = Migration.Engines.FLYWAY) to apply the db/migration scripts
Combines it with @KoraAppTest for the application graph
Does not hand-write Flyway.configure().load().migrate() boilerplate
Does not use field injection or non-Kora test bootstrap annotations in place of @KoraAppTest

evals.query: Should I pick Flyway or Liquibase for migrations in my new Kora service?
expected_behavior: Defaults to Flyway (database-flyway / FlywayJdbcDatabaseModule) as the simplest SQL-only option
Recommends Liquibase (database-liquibase / LiquibaseJdbcDatabaseModule) when declarative rollback or an existing changelog is needed
States that exactly one module should be plugged into @KoraApp
Frames the choice in Kora module terms, not third-party framework starters
